name: pip-compile-multi

metadata.name: pip-compile-multi
metadata.summary: Compile multiple requirements files to lock dependency versions

documentation:
# pip-compile-multi

Compile multiple requirements files to lock dependency versions.

## Install

```shell
pip install pip_compile_multi
```

## Run

```shell
pip-compile-multi
```

## Links

- Documentation: <https://pip-compile-multi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/>
- Releases: <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pip-compile-multi>
- Code: <https://github.com/peterdemin/pip-compile-multi>
- Issue tracker: <https://github.com/peterdemin/pip-compile-multi/issues>

# History

## 2.7.0 (2024-11-27)

- Remove Python 3.8 from tested versions
  Using Python 3.9 as the base version going forward.

## 2.6.4 (2023-06-06)

- Add `--strip-extras` pass-through flag.
  (PR [#455], thanks to [Tim Vergenz]).

## 2.6.3 (2023-05-05)

- Allow version constraints in `--upgrade-package` parameters.
  (Issue [#392], PR [#394], thanks to [Peter Law]).

## 2.6.2 (2023-02-23)

- Fix package name normalization for names with delimiters (`urltemplate` != `url-template`).

## 2.6.1 (2022-11-23)

- Add `--backtracking/--no-backtracking` flag
  ([backtracking docs](https://pip-compile-multi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/features.html#backtracking-resolver)).
  (Issue [#345], PR [#360])
- Bumped `pip-tools` minimum version constraint to `6.8.0` to support `--resolver` option.

## 2.6.0 (2022-11-23)

- Fix cross-env package names matching when they use delimiters (-\_.) and upper/lower case.

## 2.5.0 (2022-11-03)

- Add `--emit_trusted_host/--no-emit_trusted_host` flag
  ([trusted host annotation docs](https://pip-compile-multi.readthedocs.io/en/latest/features.html#add-trusted-host-annotation)).
  Thanks to [Phil Blackwood]
  (Issue [#351], PR [#353]).
- Remove Python 3.6 and 3.7 from tested versions
  (package works, but maintaining CI becomes problematic).
  Using Python 3.8 as the base version going forward.

## 2.4.6 (2022-07-22)

- Add support for [PEP-426: environment markers](https://peps.python.org/pep-0426/).

## 2.4.5 (2022-04-01)

- Fix `--build-isolation` flag passing.
  Thanks to [Jake Schmidt]
  (Issue [#312], PR [#313]).

## 2.4.4 (2022-03-30)

- Add `--build-isolation` flag.
  Thanks to [Jake Schmidt]
  (PR [#311]).
- Fix `--skip-constraints` feature for URL dependencies.

## 2.4.3 (2022-01-19)

- Prioritize URL depedencies when parsing to enable URLs that have version pins.
- Make `--autoresolve` work correctly with `-t` and `-n` options.
- Add support for "package @ url" notation.

## 2.4.2 (2021-09-01)

- Fix `--autoresolve` when `--directory="."`.

## 2.4.1 (2021-04-02)

- Add `--live/--no-live` option to control when to print debug output.
  Thanks to [John Sandall] and [Thomas Grainger]
  (Issue [#153], PRs [#247] and [#251]).
- Add `--extra-index-url <url>` option.
  Thanks to [Erik Jan de Vries]
  (PRs [#250] and [#252]).

## 2.4.0 (2021-03-17)

- Update --index/--no-index to --emit-index-url/--no-emit-index-url
  for compatibility with pip-tools 6.0
  (Issue [#243]).

## 2.3.2 (2021-02-18)

- Fix cross-feature logic for --autoresolve and --upgrade-package
  (PR [#236]).

## 2.3.1 (2021-02-16)

- Fix for a bug introduced in 2.2.2 when running pip-compile-multi
  installed for Python 3, and having `python` symlinked to Python 2
  (Issue [#233], PR [#234]).

## 2.3.0 (2021-02-04)

- Make SHA1 hashes of input files in a more robust way (Issue [#215]).
  Now it ignores changes to comments, whitespace and order of packages.

## 2.2.2 (2021-01-29)

- Add support for calling using `python -m pipcompilemulti.cli_v1` notation.

## 2.2.1 (2021-01-29)

- Add `--skip-constraints` option.
- Fix bootstrapping for autoresolve case with missing output files.

## 2.2.0 (2020-01-22)

- Add `--autoresolve` option for conflict-free compilations (PR #224).
- Auto-discover requirements in other directories by following references (PR #221).
- Add support for new-style multiline "via" comments from pip-tools (PR #222).

## 2.1.0 (2020-08-19)

- Update dependencies.
- Revert relative path normalization, introduced in #167 (thanks to @john-bodley #200).

## 2.0.0 (2020-05-18)

- Drop Python 2.7 support. pip-tools 4 no longer works with the latest pip,
  there's no way to continue Python 2.7 support.

## 1.5.9 (2020-03-23)

- Remove directory path from "via" annotations (thanks to @HALtheWise #166 #167).

## 1.5.8 (2019-09-27)

- Add option `--annotate-index` (thanks to @john-bodley #160).

## 1.5.7 (2019-09-27)

- Enable accidentially disabled `--upgrade` option.

(target-21)=

## 1.5.6 (2019-09-18)

- Minor fixes to packaging and documentation.

Warning: this version is broken and won't pass `--upgrade` option to `pip-compile`.
If you have this version installed, you need to manually upgrade it.
For example, using command:

```
pip-compile-multi --upgrade-package pip-compile-multi
```

Like in this [PR](https://github.com/mozilla-releng/shipit/pull/1).

## 1.5.4 (2019-09-16)

- Fixed MANIFEST to include features directory

Warning: this version is broken and won't pass `--upgrade` option to `pip-compile`.
See notes for [1.5.6] for details.

## 1.5.3 (2019-09-14)

- Refactored features to separate modules.
- Allow passing verify options after verify command.
- Trim irrelevant entries from the traceback.

Warning: this version is broken and won't install `features` directory.
See notes for [1.5.6] for details.

## 1.5.2 (2019-09-12)

- Added option `--allow-unsafe`. (thanks to @mozbhearsum #157).

## 1.5.1 (2019-08-08)

- Added option `--use-cache`. (thanks to @kolotev #149).

## 1.5.0 (2019-08-06)

- Changed short option for `--forbid-post` from `-P` to `-p`
  (as it conflicted with `-P` for `--upgrade-package` #147).

## 1.3.1 (2019-02-19)

- Re-removed workaround for future[s] packages in Python3

## 1.3.0 (2018-12-27)

- Introduced CLI v2 (disabled by default)

## 1.2.2 (2018-11-20)

- Removed workaround for future[s] packages in Python3 (no longer needed)

## 1.2.1 (2018-04-16)

- Fixed Restructured text formatting (thanks to @yigor)
- Updated test dependencies (and hashes)

## 1.2.0 (2018-04-03)

- Added --forbid-post option

## 1.1.12 (2018-02-23)

- Added checks for conflicting package versions
- Added support for VCS dependencies
- Added --no-upgrade option

## 1.1.11 (2018-02-09)

- Propagate --only-name option to references
- Fixed extension override options

## 1.1.10 (2018-02-09)

- Added `--generate-hashes` option

## 1.1.9 (2018-02-08)

- Fixed directory override option
- Added --only-name option

## 1.1.8 (2018-01-25)

- Fixed comment justification

## 1.1.6 (2018-01-19)

- Added `pip-compile-multi verify` command

## 1.1.5 (2018-01-16)

- Omit future[s] packages for Python3

## 1.1.0 (2018-01-12)

- Added files discovery.

## 1.0.0 (2018-01-11)

- First release on PyPI.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.actions
documentation: High level actions to be called from CLI
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.actions.recompile
documentation: Compile requirements files for all environments.

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.actions.compile_topologically
documentation: Compile environments in topological order of reference.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.utils
documentation: Functional utilities for lists and dicts manipulation.

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.utils.extract_env_name
documentation:
Return environment name for given requirements file path.

```pycon
>>> extract_env_name("base.in")
'base'
>>> extract_env_name("sub/req.in")
'req'
```

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.utils.fix_reference_path
documentation:
Find actual path to reference using relative path to original file.

```pycon
>>> fix_reference_path("dir/file", "../ref")
'ref'
```

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.utils.recursive_refs
documentation: Return set of recursive refs for given env name.

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.utils.merged_packages
documentation:
Return union set of environment packages with given names.

```pycon
>>> sorted(merged_packages(
...     {
...         'a': {'x': 1, 'y': 2},
...         'b': {'y': 2, 'z': 3},
...         'c': {'z': 3, 'w': 4}
...     },
...     ['a', 'b']
... ).items())
[('x', 1), ('y', 2), ('z', 3)]
```

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.utils.reference_cluster
documentation:
Return set of all env in_paths referencing or
referenced by given in_path.

```pycon
>>> cluster = sorted(reference_cluster([
...     {'in_path': 'base', 'refs': []},
...     {'in_path': 'test', 'refs': ['base']},
...     {'in_path': 'local', 'refs': ['test']},
... ], 'test'))
>>> cluster == ['base', 'local', 'test']
True
```

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.strip_extras
documentation:
# Strip extras

Instructs `pip-compile` to attempt to omit extras in transient dependencies,
while assuring the constraints compatibility.

```text
--strip-extras          Try avoiding use of extras.
```

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.strip_extras.StripExtras
documentation: Attempt to drop extras

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.forbid_post
documentation:
# Forbid .postX release

`pip-compile-multi` can remove `.postX` part of dependencies versions.

```text
-p, --forbid-post TEXT      Environment name (base, test, etc) that cannot
                            have packages with post-release versions
                            (1.2.3.post777).
                            Can be supplied multiple times.
```

:::{note}
Be careful with this option since different maintainers treat
post releases differently.
:::

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.forbid_post.ForbidPost

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.forbid_post.ForbidPost.enabled_envs
documentation: Convert to set.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.forbid_post.ForbidPost.drop_post
documentation:
Remove .postXXXX postfix from version.

```pycon
>>> ForbidPost.drop_post('1.2.3.post123')
'1.2.3'
>>> ForbidPost.drop_post('1.2.3')
'1.2.3'
```

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.forbid_post.ForbidPost.post_forbidden
documentation: Whether post versions are forbidden for passed environment name.
Truncate postXXX from versions for selected packages.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.unsafe
documentation:
# Allow Unsafe Packages

If your project depends on packages that include `setuptools`
or other packages considered "unsafe" by `pip-tools` and you
still wish to have them included in the resulting requirements files,
you can pass this option to do so:

```text
-s, --allow-unsafe          Whether or not to include 'unsafe' packages
                            in generated requirements files. Consult
                            pip-compile --help for more information
```

This is commonly used with --generate-hashes to avoid
generating requirements files which [cannot be installed].

[cannot be installed]: https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools/issues/806

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.unsafe.AllowUnsafe
documentation: Use pip-tools cache, or rebuild from scratch.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.base_dir
documentation:
# Requirements Directory

While it's a common practice to put requirements files inside `requirements`
directory, it's not always the case.
The directory can be overridden with this option:

```text
-d, --directory TEXT   Directory path with requirements files
```

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.base_dir.BaseDir

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.base_dir.BaseDir.path
documentation:
Get the base directory path.

```pycon
>>> BaseDir().path == 'requirements'
True
```

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.base_dir.BaseDir.file_path
documentation:
Compose file path for a given file name.

```pycon
>>> import os.path
>>> expected = os.path.join('requirements', 'base.txt')
>>> expected == BaseDir().file_path('base.txt')
True
```
Override input file extension.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_in_paths
documentation:
(limit-in-files)=

# Limit input files

By default `pip-compile-multi` compiles all `.in` files in `requirements` directory.
To limit compilation to only a subset, use

```text
-t, --only-path TEXT        Compile only for passed input file paths and
                            their references.
                            Can be supplied multiple times.
```

For example, to compile one file under Python2.7 and another under Python3.6, run:

```text
$ virtual-env27/bin/pip-compile-multi -t requirements/deps27.in
Locking requirements/deps27.in to requirements/deps27.txt. References: []
$ virtual-env36/bin/pip-compile-multi -t requirements/deps36.in
Locking requirements/deps36.in to requirements/deps36.txt. References: []
```

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_in_paths.LimitInPaths

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_in_paths.LimitInPaths.direct_envs
documentation: Set of environments included by command line options.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_in_paths.LimitInPaths.on_discover
documentation: Save set of all (recursive) included environments.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_in_paths.LimitInPaths.included
documentation: Whether environment is included directly or by reference.

Limit discovered input files to specified subset.

```pycon
>>> from pipcompilemulti.options import OPTIONS
>>> feature = LimitInPaths()
>>> OPTIONS[feature.OPTION_NAME] = ['test.in']
>>> feature.on_discover([
...     {'in_path': 'base.in', 'refs': []},
...     {'in_path': 'test.in', 'refs': ['base.in']},
...     {'in_path': 'docs.in', 'refs': []},
... ])
>>> feature.included('base.in')
True
>>> feature.included('test.in')
True
>>> feature.included('docs.in')
False
```

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_envs
documentation:
# Limit environments

:::{warning}
This flag is deprecated and will be removed in future releases.
Use {ref}`limit-in-files` instead.
:::

By default `pip-compile-multi` compiles all `.in` files in `requirements` directory.
To limit compilation to only a subset, use

```text
-n, --only-name TEXT        Compile only for passed environment names and
                            their references. Can be supplied multiple
                            times.
```

For example, to compile one file under Python2.7 and another under Python3.6, run:

```text
$ virtual-env27/bin/pip-compile-multi -n deps27
Locking requirements/deps27.in to requirements/deps27.txt. References: []
$ virtual-env36/bin/pip-compile-multi -n deps36
Locking requirements/deps36.in to requirements/deps36.txt. References: []
```

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_envs.LimitEnvs

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.limit_envs.LimitEnvs.direct_envs
documentation: Set of environments included by command line options.
Limit discovered environments to specified subset.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller
documentation: Aggregate all features in a single controller.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.bind

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.compose_output_file_path

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.drop_post
documentation: Whether post versions are forbidden for passed environment name.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.constraint
documentation: Return `~=` if package_name matches patterns, `==` otherwise.

Configure features with a list of discovered environments.

Returns a new possibly shorter env list.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.affected
documentation: Whether environment is affected by upgrade command.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.included
documentation: Whether in_path is included directly or by reference.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.get_header_text
documentation: Text to put in the beginning of each generated file.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.sink_in_path
documentation: Return input sink path if it's enabled. Otherwise None

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.sink_out_path
documentation: Return sink output path if it's enabled and exists. Otherwise None

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.process_dependency_comments
documentation: Process comments of locked dependency (e.g. # via xxx).

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.controller.FeaturesController.pipe_arguments
documentation: Values for stdout and stderr arguments to subprocess.Popen.
Gateway to a list of features.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.backtracking
documentation:
# Backtracking resolver

Pip has an option to enable [backtracking] conflict resolution logic,
which can automatically downgrade some dependencies to meet constraints
from other packages.
See also the [a note on resolvers] in `pip-compile` project.

```text
--backtracking / --no-backtracking
                            Enable backtracking resolver. Translates to
                            pip-compile --resolver=backtracking option.
```

[a note on resolvers]: https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools#a-note-on-resolvers
[backtracking]: https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/user_guide/#changes-to-the-pip-dependency-resolver-in-20-3-2020

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.backtracking.Backtracking
documentation: Enable backtracking resolver.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.file_extensions
documentation:
# Requirements Files Extensions

By default `pip-compile-multi` compiles `*.txt` from `*.in` files.
While this is a common naming pattern, each project can use it's own:

```text
-i, --in-ext TEXT      File extension of input files
-o, --out-ext TEXT     File extension of output files
```

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.file_extensions.InputExtension

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.file_extensions.InputExtension.compose_input_file_name
documentation:
Compose file name given environment name.

```pycon
>>> InputExtension().compose_input_file_name('base')
'base.in'
```
Override input file extension.

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.file_extensions.OutputExtension

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.file_extensions.OutputExtension.compose_output_file_path
documentation:
Compose file name given environment name.

```pycon
>>> OutputExtension().compose_output_file_path('sub/base.in')
'sub/base.txt'
```
Override output file extension.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features
documentation: Features as modules.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.emit_trusted_host
documentation:
(emit-trusted-host)=

# Add trusted host annotation

Control addition of trusted hosts for index URLs to generated files.
Trusted hosts can be defined in `pip.conf` file, input requirements file,
or through an argument to `pip` command.
Trusted hosts can have invalid HTTPS certificate, or use unencrypted HTTP protocol.

By default, trusted hosts are saved in the generated files.
Pass `--no-emit-trusted-host` to remove it.

If no trusted hosts are defined, this flag doesn't have any effect.

```text
--emit-trusted-host / --no-emit-trusted-host
                                Add trusted host to generated files
                                (default true)
```

See also: [annotate_index] and [extra_index_url] options.

See pip-tools issue [#382] for more details.

[#382]: https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools/issues/382

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.emit_trusted_host.EmitTrustedHost
documentation: Optionally add the trusted host to the generated files.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.skip_constraint_comments
documentation:
# Skip constraints in comments of output files

When input files contain constraint references (e.g. '-c constraints.in'),
pip-compile adds it to "via" comments. For example:

```
rsa==3.4.2
     # via
     #   -c constraints.txt
     #   google-auth
```

When this option is enabled that snippet will be converted to:

```
rsa==3.4.2
     # via google-auth
```

Saving two lines in .txt file.

```text
--skip-constraints      Remove constraints from "via" comments.
```

This feature is especially useful in combination with {ref}`autoresolve`.

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.skip_constraint_comments.SkipConstraintComments

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.skip_constraint_comments.SkipConstraintComments.enabled
documentation: Whether feature was explicitly enabled or not.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.skip_constraint_comments.SkipConstraintComments.process_dependency_comments
documentation: Remove constraint comments if feature is enabled.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.skip_constraint_comments.SkipConstraintComments._drop_sink_comment
documentation:
Erase sink constraint from comments.

```pycon
>>> feature = SkipConstraintComments()
>>> feature._drop_sink_comment("\n# via\n#   -c smth\n#   pkg\n")
'\n# via pkg'
>>> feature._drop_sink_comment("  # via pkg")
'  # via pkg'
```

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.skip_constraint_comments.SkipConstraintComments._collapse_single_via
documentation:
Combine via into a single line when it has only two lines.

```pycon
>>> feature = SkipConstraintComments()
>>> feature._collapse_single_via(["", "# via", "#   pkg"])
['', '# via pkg']
>>> feature._collapse_single_via(["  # via pkg"])
['  # via pkg']
>>> feature._collapse_single_via(["", "# via", "#   -r file"])
['', '# via -r file']
```
Remove lines like `-c file.txt` from comments in output files.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.extra_index_url
documentation:
(extra-index-url)=

# Add additional index URL to search

Pip accepts URLs for additional package indexes through `--extra-index-url`.
The same option can be passed to `pip-compile-multi` during compilation.

This option is similar to [annotate_index].
The difference is that the passed URL is not saved in any of the files,
which is helpful if the URL contains private credentials.

```text
--extra-index-url TEXT          Add additional package index URL to search
                                for package versions. Can be supplied
                                multiple times.
```

:::{warning}
Using `--extra-index-url` option to search for packages that are not in
the main repository (such as private packages) is unsafe, per a security vulnerability
called [dependency confusion]: an attacker can claim the package on
the public repository in a way that will ensure it gets chosen over the private package.

Use the `--index-url` option in pip’s configuration file or command line
to specify the feed, overriding the default.
Avoid the `--extra-index-url` option, which is additive and may lead
to having multiple indexes.

See [pip documentation] for details.
:::

[dependency confusion]: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/resources/3-ways-to-mitigate-risk-using-private-package-feeds/
[pip documentation]: https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/cli/pip_install/#examples

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.extra_index_url.ExtraIndexUrl

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.extra_index_url.ExtraIndexUrl.pin_options
documentation: Pin command options.
Forward extra index URLs to to pip-compile.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.build_isolation
documentation:
# Build isolation

Allows disabling build isolation through the equivalent `pip-compile` flag.
Build isolation is enabled by default.

```text
--build-isolation / --no-build-isolation
                                Enable isolation when building a modern
                                source distribution. Build dependencies
                                specified by PEP 518 must be already
                                installed if build isolation is disabled.
```

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.build_isolation.BuildIsolation
documentation: Proxies build isolation flag to pip-compile

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.autoresolve
documentation:
(autoresolve)=

# Autoresolve cross-file conflicts

Compile requirements file, that references all other files first,
and than use it as a constraint.

```text
--autoresolve/--no-autoresolve Automatically resolve
                               cross-file conflicts.
```

This strategy allows to resolve cross-file conflicts of two types:

1. Files FOO and BAR both have dependency PKG, but compile it to different versions.
2. FOO has PKG resolved to version 3. BAR references FOO, but also
   has another dependency, that constraints PKG to version 2.

By compiling all-including file (aka *sink*), that references both FOO and BAR first,
pip-compile-multi generates conflict-free set of versions.

After that, this compiled file is passed as a constraint for compiling
all requirements files.

As the last step, the *sink* file is compiled again preserving reference files
and skipping duplicate packages.

:::{note}
This feature works only if your project has a single requirements file,
that references (directly or indirectly) all other files.
:::

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.autoresolve.Autoresolve

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.autoresolve.Autoresolve.enabled
documentation: Whether feature was explicitly disabled or not.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.autoresolve.Autoresolve.on_discover
documentation: Save set of all (recursive) included environments.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.autoresolve.Autoresolve.sink_path
documentation: Return sink path if it's enabled. Otherwise None

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.autoresolve.Autoresolve._find_sink
documentation:
Try to find requirements sink.

Sink is a requirements file that references all other
requirement files.

If no sink exists, return None.

```pycon
>>> Autoresolve._find_sink([
...  {'in_path': 'base', 'refs': set()},
...  {'in_path': 'test', 'refs': {'base'}},
...  {'in_path': 'local', 'refs': {'test', 'base'}},
... ])
'local'
>>> Autoresolve._find_sink([
...  {'in_path': 'base', 'refs': set()},
...  {'in_path': 'test', 'refs': {'base'}},
...  {'in_path': 'doc', 'refs': set()},
... ])
>>> Autoresolve._find_sink([
...  {'in_path': 'base', 'refs': set()},
...  {'in_path': 'foo', 'refs': {'base'}},
...  {'in_path': 'bar', 'refs': {'base'}},
...  {'in_path': 'all', 'refs': {'foo', 'bar'}},
... ])
'all'
```
Detect sink file and use it unless the feature is explicitly disabled.

```text
-h, --header TEXT      File path with custom header text for generated files
```

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.use_cache
documentation:
# Use Cache

By default `pip-compile-multi` executes `pip-compile` with
`--rebuild` flag.
This flag clears any caches upfront and rebuilds from scratch.
Such a strategy has proven to be more reliable in [edge cases],
but causes significant performance degradation.

Option `--use-cache` removes `--rebuild` flag from the call
to `pip-compile`.

```text
-u, --use-cache             Use pip-tools cache to speed up compilation.
```

:::{note}
When using `--use-cache`, `pip-compile-multi` can
run **10 times faster**. But if you run into "magical" issues,
try to rerun compilation without this flag first.
:::

[edge cases]: https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools/issues?q=--rebuild

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.use_cache.UseCache
documentation: Use pip-tools cache, or rebuild from scratch.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.upgrade
documentation:
# Disable upgrades

When new dependencies are added it's tempting to keep everything else the same.
To recompile `.txt` keeping satisfying version use `--no-upgrade`:

```text
--upgrade / --no-upgrade    Upgrade package version (default true)
```

The option has no effect if there are no existing `.txt` files.

# Upgrade only selected packages

To upgrade only one package and keep everything else untouched,
use following option:

```text
-P, --upgrade-package TEXT  Only upgrade named package.
                            Can be supplied multiple times.
```

Under the hood it uses [the same option of pip-compile]
and runs compilation only for files that have one of the passed packages.

This option implies `--no-upgrade` and takes precedence over `--upgrade`.

Thanks to [Jonathan Rogers](https://github.com/JonathanRRogers).

[the same option of pip-compile]: https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools#updating-requirements

# Live output

Print debug output from pip-compile live.
If the option is disabled (by default) the debug output
is printed only in case of failure.

```text

```

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.compatible
documentation:
# Compatible Releases

[PEP-440] describes compatible release operator `~=`.
Sometimes it's useful to have some of the dependencies
pinned using this operator.
For example, rapidly changing internal libraries.
The format for this option is

```text
-c, --compatible TEXT
```

where TEXT is a [glob] pattern for library name.
This option can be supplied multiple times.

[glob]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glob_(programming)
[pep-440]: https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0440/#compatible-release

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.compatible.Compatible

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.compatible.Compatible.patterns
documentation: Use empty list as the default.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.compatible.Compatible.constraint
documentation:
Return `~=` if package_name matches patterns, `==` otherwise.

```pycon
>>> from pipcompilemulti.options import OPTIONS
>>> feature = Compatible()
>>> OPTIONS[feature.OPTION_NAME] = ['xxx']
>>> feature.constraint('package')
'=='
>>> feature.constraint('xxx')
'~='
```

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.compatible.Compatible.is_matched
documentation: Whether package name matches one of configured glob patterns.
Use ~= for selected packages.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti
documentation: Pip compile multi aka requirements

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.annotate_index
documentation:
(annotate-index)=

# Add index URL annotation

Control addition of `--index-url` options to the output files.
Corresponds to `pip-compile`'s `--emit-index-url / --no-emit-index-url` flag.

The URL can be defined in the input file as in this example:

```text
--index-url=https://pypi.tuna.tsinghua.edu.cn/simple/
six
click
```

This option is similar to [extra_index_url].
The difference is that URL is saved in the output files,
which means that it doesn't need to be configured in every environment.

```text
--annotate-index / --no-annotate-index
                                Add index URL to generated files
                                (default false)
```

Note: the default behavior is not to add the index, i.e., `--no-annotate-index`.

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.annotate_index.AnnotateIndex
documentation: Optionally annotate the index URL to the generated files.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.features.add_hashes
documentation:
# Generate hashes

Put package hash after pinned version for additional security.
Format for this option is

```text
-g, --generate-hashes TEXT  Input file name (base.in, requirements/test.in, etc)
                            that needs packages hashes.
                            Can be supplied multiple times.
                            For backwards compatibility can be short
                            environment name (base, test, etc.)
```

Example invocation:

```shell
$ pip-compile-multi -g base -g docs
```

Example output:

```text
pip-tools==1.11.0         --hash=sha256:50288eb066ce66dbef5401a21530712a93c659fe480c7d8d34e2379300555fa1         --hash=sha256:ba427b68443466c389e3b0b0ef55f537ab39344190ea980dfebb333d0e6a50a3
first==2.0.1         --hash=sha256:3bb3de3582cb27071cfb514f00ed784dc444b7f96dc21e140de65fe00585c95e         --hash=sha256:41d5b64e70507d0c3ca742d68010a76060eea8a3d863e9b5130ab11a4a91aa0e         # via pip-tools
```

`pip` requires all packages to have hashes if at least one has it.
`pip-compile-multi` will recursively propagate this option to all
environments that are referencing or referenced by selected environments.

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.features.add_hashes.AddHashes

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.add_hashes.AddHashes.enabled_in_paths
documentation:
Convert list of .in paths to a set.

For backwards compatibility, check if passed value is env name
and convert it to in_path.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.features.add_hashes.AddHashes.on_discover

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.discover
documentation: Environment discovery

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.discover.discover
documentation:
Find all files matching given glob_pattern,
parse them, and return list of environments:

Recursively follow referenced files not matched by glob_pattern.

```pycon
>>> import os
>>> envs = discover(os.path.join('requirements', '*.in'))
>>> # import pprint; pprint.pprint(envs)
>>> envs == [
...  {'in_path': os.path.join('requirements', 'base.in'), 'name': 'base',
...   'refs': set()},
...  {'in_path': os.path.join('requirements', 'test.in'), 'name': 'test',
...   'refs': {'base.in'}},
...  {'in_path': os.path.join('requirements', 'local.in'), 'name': 'local',
...   'refs': {'test.in'}},
...  {'in_path': os.path.join('requirements', 'testwin.in'), 'name': 'testwin',
...   'refs': {'test.in'}}
... ]
True
```

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.discover.order_by_refs
documentation:
Return topologicaly sorted list of environments.

I.e. all referenced environments are placed before their references.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.verify
documentation:
# Check that `pip-compile-multi` was run after changes in `.in` file

`pip-compile-multi` adds a special line (before header) at the beginning of each generated file.
This line contains a SHA1 hash of the `.in` file's contents.

Command

```shell
$ pip-compile-multi verify
Verifying that requirements/base.txt was generated from requirements/base.in.
Success - comments match.
Verifying that requirements/test.txt was generated from requirements/test.in.
Success - comments match.
Verifying that requirements/local.txt was generated from requirements/local.in.
Success - comments match.
```

recalculates hashes for `.in` files and compares them with the stored values.

If verification fails, an error message is logged and exit code 1 is returned:

```shell
$ pip-compile-multi verify
Verifying that requirements/base.txt was generated from requirements/base.in.
Success - comments match.
Verifying that requirements/test.txt was generated from requirements/test.in.
FAILURE!
Expecting: # SHA1:c93d71964e14b04f3c8327d16dbc4d6b1bbc3b1d
Found:     # SHA1:6c2562322ca1bdc8309b08581a2aa4efbb5a4534
Verifying that requirements/local.txt was generated from requirements/local.in.
Success - comments match.
```

In big teams it might be a good idea to have this check in `tox.ini`:

```ini
[testenv:verify]
skipsdist = true
skip_install = true
deps = pip-compile-multi
commands = pip-compile-multi verify
whitelist_externals = pip-compile-multi
```

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.verify.verify_environments
documentation:
For each environment verify hash comments and report failures.
If any failure occured, exit with code 1.

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.verify.generate_hash_comment
documentation:
Read file with given file_path and return string of format

    # SHA1:da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709

which is hex representation of SHA1 file content hash

functions.name: pipcompilemulti.verify.generate_robust_hash_comment
documentation:
Read file with given file_path and return string of format

    # SHA1:da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709

which is hex representation of SHA1 file content hash.
File content is pre-processed by stripping comments, whitespace and newlines.

modules.name: pipcompilemulti.dependency
documentation: Dependency class

classes.name: pipcompilemulti.dependency.Dependency

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.dependency.Dependency.serialize
documentation:
Render dependency back in string using:
    ~= if package is internal
    == otherwise

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.dependency.Dependency.without_editable
documentation:
Remove the editable flag.
It's there because pip-compile can't yet do without it
(see https://github.com/jazzband/pip-tools/issues/272 upstream),
but in the output of pip-compile it's no longer needed.

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.dependency.Dependency.drop_post
documentation: Remove .postXXXX postfix from version if needed.

Single dependency.

Comment may span multiple lines.

```pycon
>>> print(Dependency(
...   "six==1.0\n "
...   "    --hash=abcdef\n"
...   "    # via\n"
...   "    #   app\n"
...   "    #   pkg"
... ).serialize())
six==1.0 \
    --hash=abcdef
    # via
    #   app
    #   pkg
>>> print(Dependency(
...   "six==1.0\n"
...   "    # via\n"
...   "    #   app\n"
...   "    #   pkg"
... ).serialize())
six==1.0
    # via
    #   app
    #   pkg
>>> # Old-style one-line
>>> print(Dependency("six==1.0    # via pkg").serialize())
six==1.0                  # via pkg
>>> print(Dependency("-e https://site#egg=pkg==1\n   # via lib").serialize())
https://site#egg=pkg==1
   # via lib
>>> print(Dependency('dep==1 ; sys_platform == "darwin"').serialize())
dep==1 ; sys_platform == "darwin"
```

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.environment.Environment.concatenated
documentation:
Read lines from fp concatenating on backslash (\\)

```pycon
>>> env = Environment('')
>>> list(env.concatenated([
...     'pkg', 'pkg  # comment', 'pkg', '# comment', '# one more',
...     'foo', '  # via', '', '  # pkg',
... ]))
['pkg', 'pkg  # comment', 'pkg', '# comment', '# one more', 'foo', '  # via', '', '  # pkg']
```

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.environment.Environment.parse_sections
documentation:
Combine lines with following comments into sections.

```pycon
>>> env = Environment('')
>>> list(env.parse_sections([
...     'pkg', 'pkg  # comment', 'pkg', '# comment', '# one more',
...     'foo', '  # via', '', '  # pkg',
... ]))
['pkg', 'pkg  # comment', 'pkg\n# comment\n# one more', 'foo\n  # via', '\n  # pkg']
```

methods.name: pipcompilemulti.environment.Environment.fix_pin
documentation:
Fix dependency by removing post-releases from versions
and loosing constraints on internal packages.
Drop packages from ignore set

Also populate packages set
